mitre_attack: T1592.002 - Gather Victim Host Information: Software

threat.title: API Gateway Response Header Leakage
threat.description: API gateways often expose sensitive backend service information through HTTP response headers such as X-Powered-By, Server, X-AspNet-Version, and Via headers. These headers reveal technology stack details, version numbers, internal IP addresses, and backend service architecture. Attackers leverage this information for reconnaissance to identify specific vulnerabilities, plan targeted attacks, and fingerprint backend services. This is particularly dangerous in multi-tenant cloud environments where gateway misconfigurations can expose multiple backend services simultaneously.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker performs reconnaissance by sending HTTP requests to API endpoints
2. Examines response headers (Server, X-Powered-By, Via, X-AspNet-Version) to identify backend technology
3. Discovers backend service is running Kong Gateway 2.8.1 with Nginx/1.19.9
4. Finds internal IP address (10.0.15.42) leaked in Via header or error responses
5. Searches CVE databases for known vulnerabilities in identified versions
6. Launches targeted exploit against discovered vulnerabilities (e.g., CVE-2021-44228 Log4Shell if Java backend is detected)
7. Gains unauthorized access to backend services or escalates privileges
prerequisites: API gateway with default configuration exposing response headers
No response header filtering or sanitization implemented
Backend services using identifiable technology stacks
Gateway configured to pass through backend error messages

cve_references.description: Istio authorization policy bypass through HTTP request path fragments affecting API gateway routing
HTTP header smuggling vulnerability in AWS API Gateway allowing bypass of IP restrictions and cache poisoning
affected_products: AWS API Gateway (all versions prior to Nov 2021 patch)

cve_references.description: Kong Gateway exposes version information in Via header and error responses enabling targeted attacks

real_world_breaches.incident: Kong Ingress Controller Docker Image Compromise
real_world_breaches.impact: Unauthorized cryptominer image published to DockerHub affecting Kong Ingress Controller v3.4.0
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Dependabot actor confusion attack (Pwn Request exploit) leading to supply chain compromise

real_world_breaches.incident: AWS API Gateway Header Smuggling Discovery
real_world_breaches.impact: Multiple organizations vulnerable to IP restriction bypass and cache poisoning through X-Forwarded-For header manipulation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Improper header validation allowing header smuggling between gateway and backend services

real_world_breaches.impact: Reader-level users could escalate to Contributor permissions enabling full APIM configuration access
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Insufficient access controls in Direct Management API allowing privilege escalation

real_world_breaches.impact: In-the-wild exploitation of misconfigured APISIX gateways exposing admin APIs and backend service details
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Default configurations exposing admin interfaces and verbose error messages

security_research.title: Exposed Server Headers and Cybersecurity Risk
security_research.key_finding: X-Powered-By and Server headers enable attackers to identify vulnerable versions and prepare targeted exploits

mitigations.title: Configure AWS API Gateway Response Header Filtering with Mapping Templates
mitigations.description: Remove sensitive headers from API Gateway responses using response mapping templates and gateway responses. This prevents backend service information from being exposed to clients while maintaining proper API functionality.

code_example.code:
# CloudFormation template for API Gateway with secure response headers
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: 'API Gateway with secure response header configuration'

Resources:
  SecureAPIGateway:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: prod
      GatewayResponses:
        DEFAULT_4XX:
          ResponseParameters:
            gatewayresponse.header.X-Request-Id: context.requestId
            gatewayresponse.header.Server: "''"
            gatewayresponse.header.X-Powered-By: "''"
          ResponseTemplates:
            application/json: '{"message": "Client error", "requestId": "$context.requestId"}'
        DEFAULT_5XX:
          ResponseParameters:
            gatewayresponse.header.X-Request-Id: context.requestId
            gatewayresponse.header.Server: "''"
            gatewayresponse.header.X-Powered-By: "''"
          ResponseTemplates:
            application/json: '{"message": "Server error", "requestId": "$context.requestId"}'
      DefinitionBody:
        openapi: 3.0.1
        info:
          title: Secure API
          version: 1.0.0
        paths:
          /resource:
            get:
              x-amazon-apigateway-integration:
                type: aws_proxy
                httpMethod: POST
                uri:
                  Fn::Sub: arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${BackendFunction.Arn}/invocations
                responses:
                  default:
                    statusCode: 200
                    responseParameters:
                      method.response.header.Server: "''"
                      method.response.header.X-Powered-By: "''"
                      method.response.header.Via: "''"
              responses:
                '200':
                  description: Success
                  headers:
                    Server:
                      schema:
                        type: string
                    X-Powered-By:
                      schema:
                        type: string

  # Lambda function for backend
  BackendFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: nodejs18.x
      InlineCode: |
        exports.handler = async (event) => {
          return {
            statusCode: 200,
            headers: {
              'Content-Type': 'application/json',
              // Do NOT include Server, X-Powered-By headers here
            },
            body: JSON.stringify({ message: 'Success' })
          };
        };

Outputs:
  ApiEndpoint:
    Description: API Gateway endpoint URL
    Value:
      Fn::Sub: https://${SecureAPIGateway}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod/

nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
validation_steps: Deploy API Gateway with response mapping templates
Send test request: curl -I https://api.example.com/resource
Verify Server, X-Powered-By, Via headers are removed from response
Test error responses (4xx, 5xx) to ensure no information leakage
Validate that only sanitized error messages are returned

mitigations.title: Implement Kong Response Transformer Plugin for Header Removal
mitigations.description: Use Kong's response-transformer plugin to remove or override sensitive headers from backend responses. This provides centralized header management across all API routes protected by Kong Gateway.

code_example.code:
# Kong declarative configuration (kong.yml) with secure response headers
_format_version: "3.0"

# Global plugin to remove sensitive headers from all responses
plugins:
- name: response-transformer
  enabled: true
  config:
    remove:
      headers:
        - "Server"
        - "X-Powered-By"
        - "X-AspNet-Version"
        - "X-AspNetMvc-Version"
        - "X-Kong-Proxy-Latency"
        - "X-Kong-Upstream-Latency"
        - "Via"
    add:
      headers:
        - "X-Content-Type-Options:nosniff"
        - "X-Frame-Options:DENY"
        - "Strict-Transport-Security:max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"

# Service and route configuration
services:
- name: backend-api
  url: http://backend-service:8080
  routes:
  - name: api-route
    paths:
      - /api
    strip_path: false
    plugins:
    - name: response-transformer
      enabled: true
      config:
        remove:
          headers:
            - "Server"
            - "X-Powered-By"
        replace:
          headers:
            - "Server:SecureAPI"

# Additional security plugin for error handling
- name: request-termination
  enabled: true
  config:
    status_code: 503
    message: "Service temporarily unavailable"
    trigger: "error"
    echo: false  # Don't echo backend error details

iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
validation_steps: Apply Kong declarative configuration: deck sync -s kong.yml
Verify plugin is active: curl -I http://localhost:8001/plugins
Test API response: curl -I http://gateway.example.com/api/resource
Confirm Server and X-Powered-By headers are removed
Verify security headers (X-Content-Type-Options, etc.) are added
Test error scenarios to ensure no backend details leak

mitigations.title: Configure Azure API Management Response Policies for Header Sanitization
mitigations.description: Implement Azure APIM outbound policies to remove sensitive headers and sanitize error responses. This ensures consistent header management across all APIs managed by Azure APIM.

code_example.code:
<!-- Azure APIM Policy: Global outbound policy for header sanitization -->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <base />
        <!-- Additional inbound policies -->
        <set-header name="X-Request-ID" exists-action="override">
            <value>@(context.RequestId)</value>
        </set-header>
    </inbound>
    <backend>
        <base />
    </backend>
    <outbound>
        <base />
        
        <!-- Remove sensitive backend headers -->
        <set-header name="Server" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="X-Powered-By" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="X-AspNet-Version" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="X-AspNetMvc-Version" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="X-SourceFiles" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="Via" exists-action="delete" />
        
        <!-- Add security headers -->
        <set-header name="X-Content-Type-Options" exists-action="override">
            <value>nosniff</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="X-Frame-Options" exists-action="override">
            <value>DENY</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="Strict-Transport-Security" exists-action="override">
            <value>max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="Content-Security-Policy" exists-action="override">
            <value>default-src 'self'</value>
        </set-header>
        
        <!-- Sanitize error responses -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(context.Response.StatusCode >= 400)">
                <set-body>@{
                    return new JObject(
                        new JProperty("error", new JObject(
                            new JProperty("code", context.Response.StatusCode),
                            new JProperty("message", "An error occurred processing your request"),
                            new JProperty("requestId", context.RequestId)
                        ))
                    ).ToString();
                }</set-body>
                <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
                    <value>application/json</value>
                </set-header>
            </when>
        </choose>
    </outbound>
    <on-error>
        <base />
        
        <!-- Remove headers even on error -->
        <set-header name="Server" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="X-Powered-By" exists-action="delete" />
        
        <!-- Return sanitized error -->
        <set-body>@{
            return new JObject(
                new JProperty("error", new JObject(
                    new JProperty("code", 500),
                    new JProperty("message", "Internal server error"),
                    new JProperty("requestId", context.RequestId)
                ))
            ).ToString();
        }</set-body>
        <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
            <value>application/json</value>
        </set-header>
    </on-error>
</policies>

nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Apply policy at Global or API level in Azure Portal
Test API endpoint: Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://apim.example.com/api/resource -Method GET
Verify removed headers: Check response headers for absence of Server, X-Powered-By
Test error handling: Trigger 4xx/5xx errors and verify sanitized responses
Validate security headers are present: X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security
Review APIM Analytics for policy execution metrics

mitigations.title: Nginx Configuration for Header Suppression
mitigations.description: Configure Nginx (often used as reverse proxy or within Kong/OpenResty) to suppress version information and remove sensitive headers at the proxy layer.
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
PR.PT-3 - The principle of least functionality is incorporated
validation_steps: Install headers-more-nginx-module: apt-get install nginx-extras
Update /etc/nginx/nginx.conf with secure configuration
Test configuration: nginx -t
Reload Nginx: systemctl reload nginx
Verify headers: curl -I https://api.example.com/api/resource
Confirm Server header shows only 'nginx' or is removed entirely
Test error pages to ensure no version/path disclosure

indicators: Multiple requests with User-Agent strings indicating security scanners (Nmap, Nikto, Burp Suite)
Sequential requests to common API endpoints from single IP (reconnaissance pattern)
Requests specifically targeting error-inducing endpoints to trigger verbose error messages
Unusual OPTIONS/TRACE/HEAD requests attempting to elicit server information
Requests with invalid Host headers attempting to trigger server version disclosure
Rapid scanning of multiple API endpoints within short time window
Requests followed by exploitation attempts matching discovered backend technology

log_queries.query:
// Detect reconnaissance patterns targeting API Gateway
AzureDiagnostics
| where Category == "ApplicationGatewayAccessLog" or Category == "ApiManagementGatewayLogs"
| where httpMethod_s in ("OPTIONS", "TRACE", "HEAD") or requestUri_s contains "../" or requestUri_s contains "%2e%2e"
| extend ScannerPattern = iff(userAgent_s matches regex @"(nmap|nikto|burp|sqlmap|metasploit|masscan|nuclei|ffuf)", true, false)
| where ScannerPattern == true or httpStatus_d >= 400
| summarize 
    RequestCount = count(),
    UniqueEndpoints = dcount(requestUri_s),
    ErrorRate = countif(httpStatus_d >= 400) * 100.0 / count(),
    DistinctMethods = make_set(httpMethod_s)
    by clientIP_s, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where RequestCount > 20 or UniqueEndpoints > 10 or ErrorRate > 50
| order by RequestCount desc

index=api_gateway sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch:apigateway OR sourcetype=nginx:access
| eval is_scanner=if(match(user_agent, "(?i)(nmap|nikto|burp|sqlmap|nuclei|ffuf|masscan)"), 1, 0)
| eval is_recon_method=if(match(http_method, "(?i)(OPTIONS|TRACE|HEAD)"), 1, 0)
| eval is_error=if(status>=400, 1, 0)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats 
    count as total_requests,
    dc(uri_path) as unique_paths,
    sum(is_scanner) as scanner_requests,
    sum(is_recon_method) as recon_requests,
    sum(is_error) as error_count,
    values(user_agent) as user_agents
    by src_ip, _time
| eval error_rate=(error_count/total_requests)*100
| where total_requests > 20 OR unique_paths > 10 OR scanner_requests > 5 OR error_rate > 50
| table _time, src_ip, total_requests, unique_paths, error_rate, scanner_requests, user_agents
| sort -total_requests

event.category: "web" AND (event.dataset: "aws.apigateway" OR event.dataset: "nginx.access")
AND (
  http.request.method: ("OPTIONS" OR "TRACE" OR "HEAD")
  OR user_agent.original: (*nmap* OR *nikto* OR *burp* OR *sqlmap* OR *nuclei* OR *ffuf*)
  OR http.response.status_code: [400 TO 599]
)
| stats 
    count() as request_count,
    cardinality(url.path) as unique_endpoints,
    sum(case(http.response.status_code >= 400, 1, 0)) as error_count
    by source.ip, bucket(@timestamp, 5m)
| where request_count > 20 OR unique_endpoints > 10 OR error_count > 10

# CloudWatch Logs Insights query for API Gateway reconnaissance detection
fields @timestamp, @message, requestId, ip, httpMethod, status, userAgent
| filter status >= 400 or httpMethod in ["OPTIONS", "TRACE", "HEAD"]
| filter userAgent like /(?i)(nmap|nikto|burp|sqlmap|nuclei|ffuf|masscan)/
| stats 
    count() as total_requests,
    count_distinct(path) as unique_paths,
    sum(case when status >= 400 then 1 else 0 end) as errors
    by ip, bin(5m) as time_window
| filter total_requests > 20 or unique_paths > 10 or errors > 10
| sort total_requests desc

tools.name: ModSecurity WAF
tools.usage: Deploy ModSecurity rules to detect and block reconnaissance patterns including scanner User-Agents and abnormal request patterns

tools.name: AWS WAF
tools.usage: Configure AWS WAF rules to rate-limit and block requests matching scanner signatures or excessive error-inducing patterns

tools.name: Azure Application Gateway WAF
tools.usage: Enable OWASP Core Rule Set (CRS) 3.2+ to detect reconnaissance and information disclosure attempts

tools.name: Wazuh HIDS
tools.usage: Monitor API gateway logs for patterns indicating version scanning and reconnaissance activity

cve_validation.rationale: Multiple validated CVEs including AWS API Gateway header smuggling (2021), Istio CVE-2021-39156, and Kong version disclosure issues. Azure APIM vulnerabilities documented in 2024.

breach_evidence.rationale: Real-world incidents documented: Kong Ingress Controller compromise (Dec 2024), AWS API Gateway header smuggling discovery (2021), Azure APIM privilege escalation (2024), Apache APISIX exploitation (2023).

expert_review.rationale: OWASP API Security Top 10 2023 includes Security Misconfiguration (API8:2023). Trend Micro research on Kong misconfigurations. UpGuard analysis of exposed headers risk.

code_testing.rationale: Mitigation code examples provided for AWS API Gateway, Kong, Azure APIM, and Nginx. Validation steps included but not independently tested in production environments.

bug_bounty.rationale: HackerOne reports exist for nginx version disclosure (report #783852, $2,500 bounty) and api.data.gov leakage (report #266449, $1,500 bounty), demonstrating real-world exploitation.
